name: Chicken Parmesan

ingredients.item: can San Marzano tomatoes (I used whole crushed) plus one can of water
cloves garlic crushed (always remember to remove crushed garlic)

method: In a large frying pan on medium heat, add 2 T olive oil, add onion, and saute until transparent. Add crushed garlic, tomatoes, water, salt, pepper, and torn basil leaves. Stir and cover. Cover and let simmer on low heat for 20 minutes. Prepare the chicken breasts. Pound chicken breasts to 1/4 inch thin. In a small bowl, combine breadcrumbs, grated parmesan, chopped parsley, and salt & pepper. In a separate bowl beat an egg. Dip chicken in the beaten egg and then in the breadcrumb mixture. Set aside on a plate and continue to bread all the chicken. You can add an extra step and coat the chicken in flour first, then egg,and breadcrumbs. The flour helps the eggs stick, so the breadcrumbs are thicker. I usually only bread in the egg and then the breadcrumbs because that's how my grandma showed me.
In a large frying pan on medium heat, add 2 T of olive oil. Fry each piece until lightly browned on both sides, and set aside.

Coat an 8-inch casserole dish with cooking spray. Put 1-2 ladles of sauce on the bottom of the dish and arrange chicken breasts, then put slices of mozzarella on each piece of chicken. Pour half of the simmered sauce over the chicken, reserve the rest of the sauce in the frying pan. You can add a little more water to it if needed, you will manticare your pasta in it later. Top chicken breasts with torn basil leaves. Bake at 350° F for 20 minutes- or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.

In the meantime, boil a pot of salted water and add 300 grams of pasta, I use spaghetti. Cook until al dente. Add pasta directly to the sauce with 1-2 ladles of pasta water, mix, and manticare. Turn onto plates and place the chicken breasts and sauce on top. Top with more grated pecorino or parmesan and garnish with a torn basil leaf. Serves 2-3. Serving suggestion: fresh bread and salad. Olé
